title: crepis viscidula
intro: Crepis viscidula, also known as sticky hawk's beard, is a perennial plant species belonging to the sunflower family (Asteraceae). It is used in traditional medicine for its purported medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. The plant is believed to provide health benefits such as relieving digestive issues, reducing fever, and aiding in wound healing. Crepis viscidula can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, and tinctures for medicinal use, although more research is needed to confirm its efficacy and potential side effects.
